front: Ele adora vestir a camisa da empresa, trabalhando até tarde quase todos os dias.
pronunciation: EH-lee ah-DOH-rah vees-TEER ah kah-MEE-sah dah ehm-PREH-zah, trah-bah-lyAHN-doh ah-TEH TAHR-jee KWAH-zee TOH-doohz ooz DEE-ahz.
back: He loves to show deep loyalty to the company, working late almost every day.
context: Using 'vestir a camisa' (to wear the shirt) to mean showing loyalty, dedication, and commitment to an organization or cause.

front: Depois de anos na informalidade, ele finalmente conseguiu assinar a carteira.
pronunciation: deh-POY_Z jee AH-noohz nah eeng-fohr-mah-lee-DAH-jee, EH-lee fee-nahl-MEHN-tee kohn-seh-GHEW ah-see-NAHR ah kahr-TAY-rah.
back: After years of working informally, he finally managed to get a registered, official job.
context: Using 'assinar a carteira' (to sign the work booklet) to refer to getting officially registered, stable employment under Brazilian labor laws.

front: Não se preocupe com a concorrência; nós temos uma carta na manga para o lançamento.
pronunciation: NOHNG see pree-oh-KOO-pee kohm ah kohn-kohr-REHN-syah; NOHS TEH-mohz OO-mah KAHR-tah nah MAN-gah PAH-rah oh lhn-sah-MEHN-too.
back: Don't worry about the competition; we have an ace up our sleeve for the launch.
context: Using 'ter uma carta na manga' to mean having a secret plan, resource, or alternative ready for the right moment.

front: A diretoria resolveu cortar cabeças na área de marketing para reduzir os custos.
pronunciation: ah jee-reh-toh-REE-ah rreh-sohl-VEW kohr-TAHR kah-BEH-sahz nah AH-ryah jee MAHR-keh-teeng PAH-rah rreh-doo-ZEER ooz KOOS-toohz.
back: The board decided to lay people off in the marketing department to reduce costs.
context: Using 'cortar cabeças' (to cut heads) as a corporate slang for laying off employees or firing people.

front: Precisamos arregaçar as mangas e terminar essa tarefa antes do final do expediente.
pronunciation: pree-see-ZAH-mohz ah-rreh-gah-SAHR ahz MAN-gahz ee tehr-mee-NAHR EHS-sah tah-REH-fah AHN-teez doh fee-NAHL doh ees-peh-DJEHN-chee.
back: We need to roll up our sleeves and finish this task before the end of the shift.
context: Using 'arregaçar as mangas' to mean preparing to work hard on a task.

front: Ele tentou passar a perna no sócio, mas o contrato estava muito bem amarrado.
pronunciation: EH-lee tehn-TOH pah-SAHR ah PEHR-nah noo SOH-syoo, mahz oh kohn-TRAH-too ees-TAH-vah MWEE-too BEHNG ah-mahr-RAH-doh.
back: He tried to double-cross his partner, but the contract was extremely well drafted.
context: Another professional context for 'passar a perna', meaning to cheat or trick a business partner.

front: Ela trabalha duro de sol a sol para sustentar a família e pagar a faculdade.
pronunciation: EH-lyah trah-BAH-lyah DOO-roo jee SOHL ah SOHL PAH-rah soos-tehn-TAHR ah fah-MEE-lyah ee pah-GAHR ah fah-kool-DAH-jee.
back: She works hard from dawn to dusk to support her family and pay for college.
context: Using 'de sol a sol' (from sun to sun) to describe working relentlessly all day long.

front: O investidor resolveu abrir mão da patente para acelerar a produção da vacina.
pronunciation: oh eeng-vehs-chee-DOHR rreh-sohl-VEW ah-BREER MONG dah pah-TEHN-chee PAH-rah ah-seh-leh-RAHR ah proh-doo-SOHNG dah vah-SEE-nah.
back: The investor decided to waive the patent to speed up the production of the vaccine.
context: Using 'abrir mão' (to open hand) to mean giving up, yielding, or relinquishing a right or possession.

front: Ele sempre dá um jeito de puxar o saco do chefe para conseguir folgas extras.
pronunciation: EH-lee SEHM-pree DAH oong ZHAY-too jee poo-SHAHR oh SAH-koo doh SHEH-fee PAH-rah kohn-seh-GHEER FOHL-gahz EX-trahz.
back: He always finds a way to brown-nose the boss to get extra days off.
context: Using 'puxar o saco' (to pull the sack) to describe sucking up, brown-nosed behavior, or flattery.

front: A nova diretora quer colocar a mão na massa e ajudar no desenvolvimento do produto.
pronunciation: ah NOH-vah jee-reh-TOH-rah KEHR koh-loh-KAHR ah MONG nah MAHS-sah ee ah-zhoo-DAHR noo deez-eheng-vohl-vee-MEHN-too doh proh-DOO-too.
back: The new director wants to get her hands dirty and help with product development.
context: Using 'colocar a mão na massa' (to put hands in the dough) to mean getting actively involved in practical work.

front: Aquele projeto de lei polêmico acabou virando uma grande colcha de retalhos.
pronunciation: ah-KEH-lyah proh-ZHEH-toh jee LAY poh-LEH-mee-koh ah-kah-BOH vee-RAHN-doh OO-mah GRAN-jee KOHL-shah jee rreh-TAH-lyoohz.
back: That controversial bill ended up becoming a massive patchwork of mismatched ideas.
context: Using 'colcha de retalhos' (patchwork quilt) to describe something made up of mismatched, poorly integrated parts.

front: Não adianta tentar dar um nó em pingo d'água; as regras de segurança são claras.
pronunciation: NOHNG ah-jee-AHN-tah tehn-TAHR dahr oong NOH ehng PEENG-goo DAH-gwah; az RRAY-grahz jee seh-goo-RAN-sah sONG KLAH-rahz.
back: It's no use trying to achieve the impossible or bypass the rules; the safety regulations are clear.
context: Using 'dar nó em pingo d'água' (to tie a knot in a drop of water) to describe performing miracles, being exceptionally clever, or pulling off an impossibly tricky feat.

front: A negociação salarial está empacada; parece que estamos em um beco sem saída.
pronunciation: ah neh-goh-sya-SOHNG sah-lah-RYAHL ees-TAH ehm-pah-KAH-dah; pah-REH-see keh ees-TAH-mohz ehng oong BEH-koo sehng sah-EE-dah.
back: The salary negotiation is stuck; it feels like we are in a dead end.
context: Using 'beco sem saída' to describe a stalemate or a dead-end situation.

front: Minha chefe é muito exigente, mas ela sempre joga limpo com toda a equipe.
pronunciation: MEENG-yah SHEH-fee eh MWEE-too ee-zhee-ZHEHN-chee, mahz EH-lyah SEHM-pree ZHOH-gah LEEM-poo kohm TOH-dah ah ee-KEE-pee.
back: My boss is very demanding, but she always plays fair with the whole team.
context: Using 'jogar limpo' (to play clean) to mean acting honestly, transparently, or playing fair.

front: Ele tentou dar uma rasteira no colega para conseguir a promoção, mas foi descoberto.
pronunciation: EH-lee tehn-TOH dahr OO-mah rrahs-TAY-rah noo koh-LEH-gah PAH-rah kohn-seh-GHEER ah proh-moh-ZOHNG, mahz FOY dees-koh-BEHR-too.
back: He tried to backstab his colleague to get the promotion, but he was found out.
context: Using 'dar uma rasteira' (to trip someone up) to mean betraying, backstabbing, or sabotaging someone.

front: Nosso estoque está no limite; estamos trabalhando com a corda no pescoço.
pronunciation: NOHS-sooh ees-TOH-kee ees-TAH noo lee-MEE-chee; ees-TAH-mohz trah-bah-lyAHN-doh kohm ah KOHR-dah noo pehs-KOH-soo.
back: Our inventory is at its limit; we are working under extreme pressure.
context: Using 'com a corda no pescoço' (with the rope around the neck) to mean being in a highly pressured, desperate, or heavily constrained situation.

front: Você precisa pisar no freio com os gastos antes que a empresa fique sem caixa.
pronunciation: voh-SEH pree-SEE-zah pee-ZAHR noo FRAY-oo kohm ooz GAHS-toohz AHN-teez keh ah ehm-PREH-zah FEE-kee sehng KAY-shah.
back: You need to slow down on spending before the company runs out of cash.
context: Using 'pisar no freio' (to step on the brake) to mean slowing down actions or reducing expenditures.

front: Fomos pegos de surpresa e tivemos que apagar incêndios o dia inteiro na TI.
pronunciation: FOH-mohz PEH-goohz jee soor-PREH-zah ee chee-VEH-mohz keh ah-pah-GAHR eeng-SEHN-dyoohz oh DEE-ah een-TAY-roo nah cheh-EE.
back: We were caught off guard and had to put out fires all day long in IT.
context: Using 'apagar incêndio' (to put out a fire) metaphorically to describe solving urgent, unexpected problems instead of focusing on planned work.

front: Ele adora vender fumaça, prometendo lucros irreais para atrair pequenos investidores.
pronunciation: EH-lee ah-DOH-rah vehn-DEHR foo-MAH-sah, proh-meh-TEHN-doh LOO-kroohz ee-rre-AY_Z PAH-rah ah-trah-EER peh-KEH-noohz eeng-vehs-chee-DOH-reez.
back: He loves to sell hot air, promising unrealistic profits to attract small investors.
context: Using 'vender fumaça' (to sell smoke) to mean selling deceptive, empty promises or non-existent value.

front: A equipe de design conseguiu matar a charada e resolveu o bug visual do aplicativo.
pronunciation: ah ee-KEE-pee jee jee-ZAYN kohn-seh-GHEW mah-TAHR ah shah-RAH-dah ee rreh-sohl-VEW oh BUG vee-ZWAHL doh ah-plee-kah-CHEE-voo.
back: The design team managed to solve the puzzle and fixed the app's visual bug.
context: Using 'matar a charada' (to kill the riddle) to mean cracking a problem, figuring out a mystery, or finding a solution.

front: Ficamos de mãos atadas quando a diretoria congelou todos os investimentos do setor.
pronunciation: fee-KAH-mohz jee MAHEEZ ah-TAH-dahz KWAHN-doh ah jee-reh-toh-REE-ah kohn-zheh-LOH TOH-doohz ooz eeng-vehs-chee-MEHN-toohz doh seh-TOHR.
back: Our hands were tied when the board froze all investments in the sector.
context: Using 'ficar de mãos atadas' (to have one's hands tied) to mean being powerless to act or help.

front: O analista júnior deu um tiro no pé ao questionar a autoridade do gerente na frente de todos.
pronunciation: oh ah-nah-LEES-tah ZHOO-nyohr DEW oong CHEE-roo noo PEH_oo kwes-chyo-NAHR ah ow-toh-ree-DAH-jee doh zheh-REHN-chee nah FREHN-chee jee TOH-doohz.
back: The junior analyst shot himself in the foot by questioning the manager's authority in front of everyone.
context: Using 'dar um tiro no pé' to describe an action that backfires and harms oneself.

front: Ela sempre tenta levar a água para o seu moinho durante as discussões de orçamento.
pronunciation: EH-lyah SEHM-pree TEHN-tah leh-VAHR ah AH-gwah PAH-rah oh SEW moy-EENG-yoo doo-RAHN-tee az dees-koo-SOHNG_Z jee ohr-sah-MEHN-too.
back: She always tries to bring grist to her mill during budget discussions.
context: Using 'levar água para o seu moinho' (to bring water to one's mill) to mean steering situations to one's own advantage.

front: Se você continuar batendo cabeça desse jeito, o projeto nunca será entregue.
pronunciation: see voh-SEH kohn-chee-NWAHR bah-TEHN-doh kah-BEH-sah DEHS-see ZHAY-too, oh proh-ZHEH-toh NOONG-kah seh-RAH een-TREH-ghee.
back: If you keep running around in circles like that, the project will never be delivered.
context: Using 'bater cabeça' (to bump heads) to mean acting in an uncoordinated, confused, or inefficient manner.

front: O fechamento do contrato foi um divisor de águas para o crescimento da startup.
pronunciation: oh feh-shah-MEHN-too doh kohn-TRAH-too FOY oong jee-vee-ZOHR jee AH-gwah_PAH-rah oh krehs-see-MEHN-too dah s-tahr-TUP.
back: The signing of the contract was a turning point for the startup's growth.
context: Using 'divisor de águas' (watershed / water divider) to denote a major turning point or milestone.

front: Não perca tempo tentando reinventar a roda; use os templates prontos que já temos.
pronunciation: NOHNG PEHR-kah TEHM-poo tehn-TAHN-doh rray-eheng-vehn-TAHR ah RROH-dah; OO-zee ooz tem-PLAY-cheez PROHN-toohz keh ZHAH TEH-mohz.
back: Don't waste time trying to reinvent the wheel; use the ready-made templates we already have.
context: The universal idiom of avoiding unnecessary effort to recreate what already exists.

front: Eles estão tentando tapar o sol com a peneira ocultando as falhas de segurança do sistema.
pronunciation: EH-leez ees-TOHNG tehn-TAHN-doh tah-PAHR oh SOHL kohm ah peh-NAY-rah oh-kool-TAHN-doh ahz FAH-lyahz jee seh-goo-RAN-sah doh sees-TEH-mah.
back: They are trying to cover up the cracks by hiding the system's security flaws.
context: Another technical/business application of 'tapar o sol com a peneira'.

front: No início da carreira, tive que comer o pão que o diabo amassou para conseguir espaço.
pronunciation: noo ee-NEE-syoo dah kahr-RAY-rah, CHEE-vee keh koh-MEHR oh PONG keh oh djah-boo ah-mahs-SOH PAH-rah kohn-seh-GHEER ees-PAH-soo.
back: At the start of my career, I had to go through hell to find my place.
context: Using 'comer o pão que o diabo amassou' (to eat the bread kneaded by the devil) to mean going through extreme hardships or suffering.

front: O palestrante conseguiu prender a atenção do público do começo ao fim e deu um show.
pronunciation: oh pah-lehs-TRAHN-chee kohn-seh-GHEW prehn-DEHR ah ah-tehn-SOHNG doh POO-blee-koh doh koh-MEH-soo AH-oo FEENG ee DEW oong SHOW.
back: The speaker managed to hold the audience's attention from start to finish and put on an amazing performance.
context: Using 'dar um show' to mean putting on a stellar performance or handling a situation brilliantly.

front: Com a inflação alta, as famílias precisam fazer malabarismo para fechar as contas.
pronunciation: kohm ah eeng-flah-SOHNG OWL-tah, az fah-MEE-lyahz pree-see-ZAHNG fah-ZEHR mah-lah-bah-REES-moo PAH-rah feh-SHAHR az KOHN-tahz.
back: With high inflation, families need to juggle to make ends meet.
context: Using 'fazer malabarismo' (to juggle / perform acrobatics) to describe managing tight budgets or resources creatively.

front: A contratação do novo desenvolvedor foi um tiro certeiro para acelerar as entregas.
pronunciation: ah kohn-trah-tah-SOHNG doh NOH-voo deez-eheng-vohl-vee-DOHR FOY oong CHEE-roo sehr-TAY-roo PAH-rah ah-seh-leh-RAHR ahz een-TREH-gahz.
back: Hiring the new developer was a perfect hit to speed up deliveries.
context: Using 'um tiro certeiro' (a highly accurate shot) to mean a perfect, highly successful decision.

front: A gerência resolveu fazer vista grossa para as regras de vestimenta durante o verão.
pronunciation: ah zheh-REHN-syah rreh-sohl-VEW fah-ZEHR VEES-tah GROHS-sah PAH-rah az RRAY-grahz jee ves-chee-MEHN-tah doo-RAHN-tee oh veh-RONG.
back: Management decided to turn a blind eye to dress code regulations during the summer.
context: Using 'fazer vista grossa' in a corporate context to mean relaxing or ignoring rules temporarily.

front: Para lançar o aplicativo de graça, tivemos que vender a alma ao diabo com anúncios.
pronunciation: PAH-rah lhn-SAHR oh ah-plee-kah-CHEE-voo jee GRAH-sah, chee-VEH-mohz keh vehn-DEHR ah OWL-mah AH-oo DJAH-boo kohm ah-NOON-syoohz.
back: To launch the app for free, we had to sell our souls to the devil with ads.
context: A classic metaphor ('vender a alma ao diabo') representing compromising values or peace for practical gains.

front: A negociação foi difícil, mas no final, conseguimos fechar com chave de ouro.
pronunciation: ah neh-goh-sya-SOHNG FOY jee-FEE-seeo, mahz noo fee-NAHL, kohn-seh-GHEE-moohz feh-SHAHR kohm SHAH-vee jee OW-roo.
back: The negotiation was difficult, but in the end, we managed to close on a high note.
context: Using 'fechar com chave de ouro' (to close with a golden key) to mean ending a process beautifully or with great success.

front: Ela tentou dar um golpe do baú casando-se com o herdeiro da grande construtora.
pronunciation: EH-lyah tehn-TOH dahr oong GOHL-pee doh bah-OO kah-ZAHN-doh-see kohm oh ehr-DAY-roo dah GRAN-jee kohn-stroo-TOH-rah.
back: She tried to marry for money by marrying the heir to the major construction company.
context: Using 'dar o golpe do baú' (to pull the trunk heist) to mean marrying someone strictly for financial gain.

front: O relatório de vendas está uma bagunça; você vai ter que suar a camisa para corrigir tudo.
pronunciation: oh rreh-lah-TOH-ryoo jee VEHN-dahz ees-TAH OO-mah bah-GOON-sah; voh-SEH VAY TEHR keh SWAHR ah kah-MEE-sah PAH-rah kohr-ree-ZHEER TOO-doh.
back: The sales report is a mess; you will have to sweat your guts out to correct everything.
context: Using 'suar a camisa' (to sweat the shirt) to mean working extremely hard or putting in intense physical/mental effort.

front: Não adianta tentar fazer média com o investidor; ele só se importa com números reais.
pronunciation: NOHNG ah-jee-AHN-tah tehn-TAHR fah-ZEHR MEH-dyah kohm oh eeng-vehs-chee-DOHR; EH-lee SOH see eem-POHR-tah kohm NOO-meh-roohz rre-AY_Z.
back: It is no use trying to curry favor with the investor; he only cares about real numbers.
context: Using 'fazer média' in an investor relation context.

front: No nosso departamento, todos precisam jogar no mesmo time para atingir a meta.
pronunciation: noo NOHS-sooh dee-pahr-tah-MEHN-too, TOH-doohz pree-see-ZAHNG zho-GAHR noo MEHS-moo CHEE-mee PAH-rah ah-cheeng-ZHEER ah MEH-tah.
back: In our department, everyone needs to play on the same team to hit the target.
context: Using 'jogar no mesmo time' (to play on the same team) to emphasize collaboration and unity.

front: A demissão em massa deixou toda a equipe pisando em ovos no escritório.
pronunciation: ah deh-mee-SOHNG ehng MAHS-sah day-SHOH TOH-dah ah ee-KEE-pee pee-ZAHN-doh ehng OH-voohz noo ees-kree-TOH-ryoo.
back: The mass layoff left the entire team walking on eggshells in the office.
context: Another high-stress workplace application for 'pisando em ovos'.

front: Ele adora colher os louros do trabalho dos outros sem ter escrito uma linha de código.
pronunciation: EH-lee ah-DOH-rah koh-LYEHR ooz LOH-roohz doh trah-BAH-lyoo doohz OW-troohz sehng TEHR ees-KREE-too OO-mah LEENG-yah jee KOH-jee-goo.
back: He loves to reap the laurels of other people's work without having written a single line of code.
context: Using 'colher os louros' (to reap the laurels) to mean taking credit or enjoying the rewards of an achievement.

front: Nosso concorrente direto deu com os burros n'água ao lançar o produto sem testes.
pronunciation: NOHS-sooh kohn-kohr-REHN-chee jee-REH-too DEW kohm ooz BOO-rroohz NAH-gwah AH-oo lhn-SAHR oh proh-DOO-too sehng TEHS-cheez.
back: Our direct competitor fell flat on their face by launching the product without testing.
context: Using 'dar com os burros n'água' (to hit the water with the donkeys) to mean failing completely in an undertaking or plan.

front: Ela tentou chover no molhado repetindo os mesmos argumentos na reunião de ontem.
pronunciation: EH-lyah tehn-TOH sho-VEHR noo moh-LYAH-doh rreh-peh-CHEENG-doh ooz MEHS-moohz ahr-goo-MEHN-toohz nah rrehoo-nee-ONG jee OHN-cheh.
back: She tried to beat a dead horse by repeating the same arguments in yesterday's meeting.
context: Using 'chover no molhado' to emphasize redundant, useless talk in meetings.

front: Ele pensou que ia nos enganar com facilidade, mas acabou caindo do cavalo feio.
pronunciation: EH-lee pehn-SOH keh EE-ah nohz eheng-gah-NAHR kohm fah-see-lee-DAH-jee, mahz ah-kah-BOH kah-EENG-doh doh kah-VAH-loo FAY-oo.
back: He thought he was going to trick us easily, but he ended up failing miserably.
context: Using 'caindo do cavalo feio' to describe a sudden, humiliating failure.

front: A equipe teve que rebolar para conseguir entregar a apresentação no prazo apertado.
pronunciation: ah ee-KEE-pee CHEE-vee keh rreh-boh-LAHR PAH-rah kohn-seh-GHEER een-treh-GAHR ah ah-preh-zehn-tah-ZOHNG noo PRAH-zoo ah-pehr-TAH-doh.
back: The team had to bend over backward to manage to deliver the presentation within the tight deadline.
context: Using 'rebolar' (literally to sway hips / dance samba) to mean doing frantic maneuvers, improvisations, or working intensely to solve a difficult situation.

front: Não perca tempo tentando tapar o buraco do orçamento com soluções temporárias.
pronunciation: NOHNG PEHR-kah TEHM-poo tehn-TAHN-doh tah-PAHR oh boo-RAH-koo doh ohr-sah-MEHN-too kohm soh-loo-SOHNG_Z tehm-poh-RAH-ryahz.
back: Don't waste time trying to temporarily patch the budget deficit with temporary solutions.
context: Using 'tapar o buraco' to refer to a quick, non-ideal fix for a resource deficit.
